opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is incredibly tight, I'm coughing constantly, and my blue puffer just isn't working anymore. I also wanted to sort out my asthma plan.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Wants help because the reliever puffer has stopped easing the breathlessness over the last several hours, and also wants an asthma action plan reviewed/updated.

symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 8 hours and getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.character: tight chest and a dry constant cough that has turned into real breathlessness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst my breathing has been in a long time; the puffer used to fix it and now it barely touches it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse overnight and this morning; keeps waking me up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the blue puffer helped a bit at first but not anymore
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving around the flat, and it was dusty in the bedroom earlier today
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: coughing, chest tightness, feeling puffed after only a few steps
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had asthma for years and flare-ups before, but this one isn't settling like they usually do
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm keeping to the bedroom because walking any further leaves me too breathless

past_medical_history.how_managed: a blue reliever puffer and a brown preventer puffer

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: been taking it about every two hours today and it's not helping like it should
I'll be honest I don't take it every day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; no one else in the flat overnight

objective_data_offered.value: on the video call she is audibly wheezy and can only get a few words out before pausing to breathe; she looks like she is working to breathe
she says the reliever puffer 'isn't working anymore' and that she's needed it roughly every two hours today
